data.title: Amazon Stock Climbs Amid Layoffs Chatter — Here's What Lloyd Walmsley Sees Next
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) stock opened 2026 amid renewed discussion around workforce reductions, but the stock has shown little sign of stress. Despite some headlines resurfacing around potential layoffs, shares closed the first full trading week of the year up ~6%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The muted reaction makes more sense once you filter out the rumors and stick to what Amazon has actually said. The company confirmed in late 2025 that it was eliminating about 14,000 corporate roles, or roughly 4% of its non-warehouse workforce, as part of a broader efficiency push. Reports pointing to far deeper cuts – in some cases as many as 30,000 positions – continue to circulate, but those figures have not been officially backed by Amazon.

One way or another, investors generally view layoffs at scale not as a red flag, but as a lever for margin expansion, particularly for a company already operating at Amazon's size. With consensus EPS estimates for fiscal 2026 hovering around $7.85, the question is whether efficiency gains can meaningfully lift earnings without undermining execution. This is where the bull case shifts toward AWS.

One of the most optimistic voices on the Street belongs to Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley, who sees Amazon's cloud business entering a new phase of AI-driven growth. Walmsley, who's ranked among the top 2% on Wall Street, argues that investor expectations for AWS remain too conservative, particularly as AI workloads evolve. In his view, the industry is moving away from training-heavy demand toward inference, a shift that plays directly to AWS's strengths in custom silicon and cost-efficient infrastructure.

Walmsley believes Amazon's AI-related revenue could double in 2026, reaching about $17 billion, as inference adoption accelerates and capacity constraints ease. The top analyst also points to Amazon's expanding lineup of custom chips, including Trainium and Graviton, as a key advantage, helping customers lower costs while scaling AI workloads. In his words, AWS is increasingly positioned to "support AI at scale" in a way that few competitors can match.

Importantly, Walmsley expects this momentum to extend beyond a single year. As more enterprises migrate legacy workloads to the cloud and layer AI capabilities on top, AWS growth could re-accelerate through the second half of 2026 and beyond.

That combination of cloud migration, AI inference, and operating leverage underpins Walmsley's Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on AMZN shares, while his $300 price target implies 27% upside. (To watch Walmsley's track record, click here)

Walmsley is hardly the only analyst impressed with Amazon. The stock has 46 Buy recommendations and just 1 Hold, earning it a Strong Buy consensus rating. The $294.55 average price target suggests 19% upside potential in the coming months. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 'Structural Winner of the AI Wars': Scotiabank Crowns Alphabet Stock (GOOGL)
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOGL) shares edged higher on Friday as Scotiabank proclaimed the U.S. tech giant the "structural winner of the artificial intelligence wars" among hyperscalers despite its relatively slower entry.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Scotiabank analyst Nat Schindler reaffirmed his Buy rating on Alphabet's shares and raised his price target by about 12% from $336 per share to $375, implying about 15% upside from the stock's closing price on Thursday. With gains of almost 65%, Alphabet's shares led the "Magnificent Seven" group in 2025 in terms of stock performance.

In giving his rating, Schindler emphasized the fact that GOOGL stock has climbed about 64% over the past 12 months, adding that "every decision they make [at Google] seems to be the correct one."

Scotiabank Bullish on Google Cloud

In addition, the analyst pointed to Alphabet's Wall Street-beating third-quarter earnings results that were "very strong across the board," including Search, YouTube and Cloud.

"We're particularly bullish on the potential upside within Cloud, especially on operating margin," Schindler noted, adding that he expects the tech giant's stock to outperform due to its positioning to benefit from the monetization of AI. The analyst backed his bullish view despite acknowledging that there may be "some noise" from non‑cash charges related to Google's capital assets, data centers, and servers, etc. — that is, depreciation and amortization.

Mizuho Sees AV Scaling Beyond Waymo

Meanwhile, Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley in his latest assessment of the internet sector, noted that he expects the "AI megatrend" seen in 2025 to continue this year. Walmsley anticipates focus to shift to product changes and ad monetization by OpenAI and the scaling of autonomous vehicle (AV) deployments beyond Alphabet's Waymo, the tech leader's self-driving unit.

Waymo reported industry-leading 14 million rides between January and early December 2025. Nonetheless, the five-star analyst also reaffirmed his Buy rating on Google and raised his GOOGL price target by 12% to $365, implying about 11% upside.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Alphabet's shares continue to boast a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts based on 27 Buys and six Holds issued over the past three months by 33 analysts.

However, the average GOOGL price target of $337.87 implies less than 3% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Here's Why Barclays, Wells Fargo Upgraded Airbnb Stock (ABNB)
data.description:
Wall Street analysts are seeing improving prospects for online lodging platform Airbnb (ABNB). On Friday, Barclays analyst Trevor Young upgraded Airbnb stock from Sell to Hold and increased the price target from $107 to $120. Additionally, Wells Fargo analyst Ken Gawrelski upgraded ABNB stock from Sell to Hold and lifted the price target from $118 to $128. Let's look at the reasons for the rating upgrades.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Barclays Analyst Upgrades Airbnb Stock

Young noted that Airbnb has underperformed the Nasdaq and OTA (online travel agent) peers Booking Holdings (BKNG) and Expedia (EXPE) in each of the last two years, as industry growth normalized following the post-pandemic recovery in travel demand and the company's massive growth faded.

That said, the analyst now sees "diminished downside risks" to ABNB stock and a few potential catalysts (such as the reserve now, pay later (RNPL) offering and the 2026 FIFA World Cup) that would enable Airbnb to deliver the best room-night growth among the scaled online travel peer group. However, Young cautioned about pressure on margins for some time.

Despite several positives, Young is sidelined on the stock as he thinks that Airbnb is "still largely a monoline business" and hasn't demonstrated its ability to expand into adjacent growth areas.

Reasons for Wells Fargo's Rating Upgrade

Gawrelski stated that his rating upgrade for ABNB stock follows two years of underperformance, along with several upside possibilities, such as enhanced hotel supply and sponsored listings opportunities. The 4-star analyst views relative insulation from artificial intelligence (AI) risk as an opportunity.

Also, Gawrelski sees limited downside risk to estimates, given that the company is 12 months or more into the reinvestment period, with limited market expectations for payoff, a healthy travel industry forecast, and the expansion of the RNPL offering.

The upgrade by analysts at Barclays and Wells Fargo followed the rating upgrade by Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Deepak Mathivanan to Hold from Sell, with a price target of $141, up from $117. The 5-star analyst believes that ABNB stock's valuation is "more accommodating" following its underperformance in 2025.

Is ABNB Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Airbnb stock based on 11 Buys, 16 Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average ABNB stock price target of $143.53 indicates a modest upside potential of 3.1%. ABNB stock has risen 6.5% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 'It's Years Away,' Says Morgan Stanley on Tesla Stock as Nvidia Reframes the Autonomy Race
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) isn't the world's top EV seller anymore. That title now belongs to China's BYD, which overtook Tesla on sales volume in 2025. Falling deliveries at Tesla have been well known for a while, but that hasn't really shaken investor confidence. Instead, the focus has shifted to what's next – AI, autonomy, and Full Self-Driving – with many betting that this is where Tesla ultimately pulls ahead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

But that confidence may be getting tested.

At CES, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) made the case that the next major leap in AI won't be confined to data centers. Instead, autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots took center stage, with much of the attention landing on Alpamayo – Nvidia's vision-language-action model that, while first unveiled in October 2025, received its biggest spotlight during the keynote.

Alpamayo is built to tackle the "long tail" of autonomous driving – the rare, unpredictable real-world scenarios that are hard to train for but often determine whether a system is truly safe and reliable. To support that push, Nvidia paired the model with AlpaSim, an open-source autonomous vehicle simulation platform, and Physical AI Open Datasets featuring more than 1,700 hours of driving data, all aimed at helping automakers move closer to scalable, reasoning-based autonomy. Nvidia also used the stage to highlight Isaac GR00T N1.6, a reasoning-focused vision-language-action model designed specifically for humanoid robots.

"Understandably," says Morgan Stanley's Andrew Percoco, "the focus on AV and Alpamayo created some concern around TSLA's FSD/robotaxi competitive moat. That said, we believe Tesla is years ahead of competitors when it comes to autonomy with a clear data and scale advantage."

Percoco, who's ranked among the top 3% on Wall Street, thinks Nvidia's autonomous driving tech stack provides auto OEMs with a "capital efficient" pathway into more advanced autonomy, which for many could help narrow the gap in remaining competitive as L2+/L3 capabilities become a "standard consumer expectation."

But while Nvidia's solutions could enable OEMs to jump-start their autonomy initiatives, the time required to build out a complete AV stack and integrate it into a production vehicle – including packaging, power, sensors, mounting, time synchronization, and safety validation, as well as reaching acceptable cost levels – is likely to span years rather than months.

"None of this materially changes our Tesla outlook," says Percoco, "as our base case already embeds widespread L2+/FSD equivalent adoption across the industry over the next 10+ years. NVIDIA's product offering simply arms more OEMs with a faster follower strategy."

As for humanoids, Nvidia's stack could "significantly shorten" early-stage training cycles, but real-world deployment constraints – including integration, cost, industrial validation, and manufacturing – "remain the gating factor," areas where Tesla likely continues to hold an advantage.

Bottom line, there's no change to Percoco's Equal-weight (i.e., Neutral) rating on TSLA shares, nor to his $425 price target, which sits 4% below the current share price. (To watch Percoco's track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 13 Buys, 9 Holds, and 7 Sells, for a Hold consensus rating. Going by the $405.94 average price target, shares will be changing hands at a ~9% discount a year from now. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.description:

data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY Secures Record High on Unemployment Rate Drop and Rising Consumer Sentiment
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished the second week of 2026 on a high note, with the former clinching a new record-high close of $694.07.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This morning, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that the unemployment rate was 4.4% in December, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. In addition, the BLS revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

It wasn't all good news, as December's nonfarm payrolls clocked in at 50,000, which was below the estimate of 70,000. On top of that, October and November's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 76,000 jobs. For 2025, the U.S. economy added 584,000 jobs, registering the worst year for job gains outside of a recession since 2003.

"You don't want to become unemployed, because if you do, it's very difficult to get back in," said Moody's Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi. "The duration of unemployment is now becoming quite long." Rate cuts can stimulate the labor market, although the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the January 28 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting more than halved to 5% from 11.1% a day ago on CME's FedWatch tool.

Elsewhere, the University of Michigan announced that January's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment was 54, higher than the estimate of 53.5 and improving from 52.9 in December. At the same time, long-run inflation expectations rose by 0.2% to 3.4% and surpassed the range of 2.8% to 3.2% in 2025. Year-ahead inflation expectations remained unchanged at 4.2%, the lowest level since January 2025.

"Although consumers' worries about tariffs appear to be gradually receding, they remain guarded about the overall strength of business conditions and labor markets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Inflation is still a key risk for the economy, said Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic in an interview with the WLRN radio station. Bostic noted that the Fed must remain "laser-focused" on lowering prices and previously stated that he would not support rate cuts in 2026 unless inflation eases.

Finally, investors expecting a Supreme Court ruling on the Trump administration's tariffs will have to wait a bit longer. SCOTUS held its opinion day at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, although the court didn't provide a decision on the case. SCOTUS has scheduled its next opinion day for Wednesday, January 14.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.65% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 1.02%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Hold the Reins,' Says Raymond James About Apple Stock
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) may boast rock-solid fundamentals and improving product cycles, but according to Raymond James analyst Melissa Fairbanks, much of that optimism is already baked into the stock. In her view, Apple's current valuation leaves little room for meaningful upside in the near term.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"While we acknowledge the company's leadership in consumer hardware, ecosystem, and services, with a highly sticky value proposition, we believe much of this value is already well understood by investors," the 5-star analyst explained. "Further, given the broad user base (now ~2.4 billion), we believe incremental gains from technology upgrade cycles will become more difficult to attain, much less move the needle."

Fairbanks' comments come amid what is widely viewed as a generally successful iPhone 17 refresh cycle, with the iPhone 17 lineup boasting features that support deeper integration of GenAI capabilities. While adoption of AI functionality at the edge is expected to remain relatively limited in the near term, Fairbanks believes Apple's on-device AI models, together with secure cloud processing, should enhance core productivity applications and Siri across the new lineup, a shift she expects will "be a catalyst for a healthy upgrade cycle after a number of generally lackluster years."

But with the stock up by 23% over the past 6 months, driven by the "relative success" of the refresh cycle, Fairbanks is concerned about a valuation "several turns above the 5-year average P/E."

Moreover, while the momentum seen in the Services business is encouraging, the investment case is "still a hardware story." No doubt, the traction of Apple Services is a real positive, representing ~26% of FY25 revenue and expected to remain the main growth driver on a percentage basis, helping to offset the "highly seasonal" nature of hardware sales through a higher-margin, recurring revenue stream. Additionally, Apple's software ecosystem continues to address large end markets, reducing the likelihood of users migrating to alternative platforms. Even though Fairbanks sees the integration of services with hardware as a "positive element" of the narrative, she expects iPhone upgrade cycles to remain the primary driver of "broader returns," and therefore anticipates the share price will continue to reflect this cyclicality, albeit within an "overall upward trend."

"While we acknowledge AAPL is largely viewed as a core holding, we see limited near-term catalysts to drive meaningful outperformance versus our coverage universe," the analyst summed up.

To this end, Fairbanks assigns AAPL stock a Market Perform (i.e., Neutral) rating without having a fixed price target in mind. (To watch Fairbanks' track record, click here)

Other analysts do have numbers in mind, and their average price target of $299.49 points to ~15% upside over the next year. In total, 11 analysts sit on Hold, but with 19 Buys and just 2 Sells, AAPL still earns a Moderate Buy consensus rating. (See AAPL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Begins Selling Novo Nordisk's Weight-Loss Pill
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) has announced that it is now selling Novo Nordisk's (NVO) new weight-loss pill through its digital pharmacy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Novo Nordisk began rolling out an oral version of its injectable obesity drug Wegovy in the U.S. on Jan. 5, offering a starting dose for $149 a month. Patients with insurance coverage can get the pill for as low as $25 per month.

The daily pill is the newest product in the red-hot market for weight-loss and diabetes drugs, which is dominated by Denmark's Novo Nordisk and U.S.-based Eli Lilly (LLY). The availability of the new Wegovy pill on Amazon highlights Novo Nordisk's efforts to expand beyond traditional sales channels and reach new patients as it scrambles to gain market share.

Beating Eli Lilly To Market

Amazon said that in the coming weeks, it will also offer the Wegovy pill through its prescription vending kiosks, which are located in select One Medical clinics across the U.S. The Seattle-based e-commerce company launched its Amazon Pharmacy in 2020 as part of a push into the U.S. healthcare industry.

Amazon aims to attract users to its pharmacy by offering speedy delivery and price transparency. It currently offers same-day prescription delivery to nearly half of U.S. consumers. After its recent launch, Novo Nordisk's weight-loss pill is now available at more than 70,000 U.S. pharmacies, as well as Costco Wholesale (COST).

Novo Nordisk beat Eli Lilly to market with its obesity pill. Eli Lilly has a rival weight-loss pill that is slated to win U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approval later this year.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 47 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 46 Buy and one Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $294.55 implies 19.09% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Cathie Wood's ARK Makes New Biotech Bets in Her Latest Trades — Here's What It Sold
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made several portfolio moves across biotech and industrial names on Friday, January 9, 2026. The firm increased its exposure to early-stage biotech stocks, while trimming a major genomics name.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did ARK Buy on January 9?

On the buying side, ARK's largest move was a fresh add to Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA). The firm bought 216,411 shares, worth about $2.28 million, across its ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG).

The purchase highlights ARK's continued confidence in gene-editing technology, even as the biotech sector remains volatile.

Building on that, ARK also added 262,769 shares of Personalis Inc. (PSNL) through its ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). The buy, valued at roughly $2.20 million, follows earlier purchases in the week, pointing to steady accumulation.

Outside biotech, ARK increased exposure to industrial equipment by buying 49,053 shares of Komatsu Ltd. (KMTUY), worth about $1.59 million, via its ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX).
